name: Corporate Governance Collapse
description: A pattern where external reports or whistleblowers expose governance failures, leading through denial, investigation delays, board restructuring, and eventual regulatory intervention or resolution.

phases.name: External Report / Whistleblower
phases.description: An external report, investigative article, or whistleblower disclosure surfaces governance concerns. Initial market reaction is sharp but often recovers partially.

phases.name: Management Denial
phases.description: Company management issues denials, threatens legal action, and attempts to control the narrative. Supporters rally. Market partially stabilizes.

phases.name: Audit / Investigation Delays
phases.description: Promised audits or investigations are delayed. Auditors resign or are replaced. Information becomes scarce. This is often the most dangerous phase.

phases.name: Board Restructuring
phases.description: Board members resign or are replaced. New management brought in. Restructuring plans announced. Creditors and investors negotiate.

phases.name: Resolution
phases.description: Regulatory intervention, settlement, bankruptcy filing, or recovery plan execution. The story reaches a definitive turning point.
